name: FRED — Federal Reserve Economic Data
description: FRED is the St. Louis Fed's economic data warehouse — the authoritative source for ~830,000 US and international macro time series: CPI, unemployment, GDP, Fed funds rate, Treasury yields, M2, mortgage rates, dollar index, oil price, and basically every other macroeconomic indicator a trading agent might want. Free, official, and the API has been stable for over a decade. Required for any agent reasoning about Fed policy, recession risk, inflation, or other macro-driven prediction markets and equities trades. Sign up at https://fredaccount.stlouisfed.org/apikeys for a free api_key.

credential_fields.name: api_key
credential_fields.label: FRED API Key
credential_fields.description: 32-char alphanumeric key from https://fredaccount.stlouisfed.org/apikeys. Free, no rate limit cap; FRED requests you 'be reasonable'.

tools.name: series_observations
tools.description: Get the time-series values for a FRED series. The most common call — feed it a series_id like 'UNRATE' (unemployment rate), 'CPIAUCSL' (headline CPI), 'FEDFUNDS' (Fed funds rate), 'DGS10' (10-year Treasury), 'GDPC1' (real GDP), etc. Returns date+value pairs. Use limit + offset for big series; use sort_order=desc for the latest first.

series_id.description: FRED series id (UNRATE, CPIAUCSL, FEDFUNDS, DGS10, GDPC1, etc.).

observation_start.description: YYYY-MM-DD, default is the series' first observation.

observation_end.description: YYYY-MM-DD, default is the latest available.

sort_order.description: default asc (oldest first); use desc for latest first

units.description: lin=levels (default), pch=% change period-over-period, pc1=% change year-over-year, log=natural log.

frequency.description: Down-sample frequency — d=daily, w=weekly, m=monthly, q=quarterly, a=annual.

aggregation_method.description: How to aggregate when frequency is coarser than source. eop=end-of-period.

tools.name: series_search
tools.description: Find a FRED series by free-text query. Use this when the agent knows the concept (e.g. 'US unemployment rate', 'core CPI services') but not the exact series_id. Returns matches with id + title + frequency + units + last-update.

search_text.description: Free-text query — 'unemployment', '10 year treasury', 'core cpi services'.

search_type.description: full_text searches title+notes; series_id matches the id literally.

order_by.description: search_rank, series_id, title, units, frequency, observation_start, observation_end, popularity, last_updated.

tools.name: series_info
tools.description: Get the metadata for one series — title, frequency, units, observation range, last-updated, notes. Use after a search to confirm you've got the right thing before pulling observations.

tools.name: category_children
tools.description: Browse FRED's category tree — alternative to free-text search. Root category id is 0. Use this when exploring a topic ('Money, Banking, & Finance' → 'Interest Rates' → ...).

tools.name: category_series
tools.description: List every series in a given FRED category. Useful after category_children to enumerate what's available under a topic.

tools.name: release_dates
tools.description: Calendar of upcoming + recent FRED release dates — when CPI prints, when jobs report drops, etc. Critical for macro prediction markets where the agent wants to know 'when does the next number land?'.

release_id.description: FRED release id (50=CPI, 76=Employment Situation, 53=Industrial Production, etc.).

tools.name: releases
tools.description: List every release FRED tracks. Use to discover release_ids to feed release_dates.

tools.name: series_updates
tools.description: Recently-updated series across FRED. Use as a heartbeat — 'what new data landed in the last day/week?'.

start_time.description: YYYYMMDDHhmm format — observations updated on or after.
